channel.title: Production on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Production on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Creating Playtest Plans
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about creating play test plans.

item.title: A Typical Day Of Making Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a typical day of making Fallout, kind of to give you an insight into what it was like.

item.title: Adding A Feature: Then And Now
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about adding a feature to a game back in the day compared to today.

item.title: Why I Don’t Solo Dev
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about why I don’t solo dev.

item.title: Game Developer Stage Limitations
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game developer stage limitations, which is a somewhat complicated talk.

item.title: Industry Pendulum Swings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about industry pendulum swings.

item.title: "Eating Your Own Dog Food"
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about eating your own dog food.

item.title: Does More Dev Time Mean A Better Game?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about does more Dev time mean a better game?

item.title: Onboarding New Team Members
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about onboarding new team members.

item.title: When Your Game Doesn’t Sell
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about what you should do when your game doesn’t sell.

item.title: The Cost Of Moddability
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the cost of modability, specifically the cost of making your video game modable by end users.

item.title: Stress Testing Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about stress testing games.

item.title: Rising Game Budgets
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Rising Game budgets.

item.title: Adding New Features After Launch
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about adding new features after launch, and by that I mean I’m going to talk about why it can be very difficult sometimes to add a new feature after the game has shipped.

item.title: Communication Between Roles
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about communication between roles, specifically, how to handle all the different roles of people on your development team and getting them to talk to each other.

item.title: Design Fallbacks
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about design fallbacks now.

item.title: Min Specs And Ratings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game minimum specifications and ratings.

item.title: Localization Questions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about localization.

item.title: Flow
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about flow.

item.title: Creativity Under Pressure
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about creativity Under Pressure.

item.title: The Outer Worlds Vertical Slice
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I have a very special video to show you.

item.title: Arcanum Data
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Arcanum data.

item.title: Time Estimation
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about time estimation.

item.title: The Horizontal Slice
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the horizontal slice.

item.title: The Missing R&D Department
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the missing R&D department, and by R&D I mean research and development.

item.title: Common Sources Of Programming Frustration
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about common sources of programming frustration.

item.title: Fallout Development Timeline
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to do the Fallout development timeline.

item.title: Five Traits In A Good Producer
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about what makes a good producer.

item.title: How To Pick The Right Project
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how to pick the right project, and this comes from a question from Bridges, chroma 1832, who asks: can you talk about how project approval decisions are made in game companies?

item.title: Shorter Times And Smaller Budgets
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about what to do when time and budget are tight.

item.title: Game Production Stages
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game production stages.

item.title: Issues...And How To Fix Them
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I not only want to talk about issues that come up in game development, but how to fix them.

item.title: Game Development Time
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game development time.

item.title: Perfect Is The Enemy Of Good
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a common phrase I hear in the industry called perfect is the enemy of good.

item.title: Team Size
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a topic that was suggested to me by a co-worker, or I should say by someone who’s working at one of the companies I’m currently Contracting at, uh, and he asked: I’d love to hear your thoughts on the ever expanding team size on projects and the impact that has on the process of making games.

item.title: Crunch
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about crunch.

item.title: My GNW Award
item.description: I forgot to mention in my previous video that Canal was used on so many projects at interplay and saved many months of development time on those projects that I won an award. Get a load of that.

item.title: How To Build A Motivated Team (1996)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I’m going to read to you a presentation that I made — I think it was sometime in 1996 — at interplay, on how to build a motivated team.

item.title: Playtesting The Outer Worlds
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about play testing in the inner worlds.

item.title: The Trials of The Temple of Elemental Evil
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, so by now we’ve I’ve talked about troika’s games, Arcanum and bloodlines.
